summary: Docker build (buildx bake) failed because a wget download of the nydus release returned HTTP 502, causing the build step in the Dockerfile to exit with code 1.
root_cause: A network fetch during the Dockerfile build failed: wget of the nydus release returned HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway, causing the RUN step to exit with code 1 and the build to fail.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the network request to GitHub returned a 502, a transient/network error during download.
Identifies the Dockerfile RUN line that attempted the failing download (the exact command that failed).

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The buildx/buildkit error showing the wget step exited with a non-zero code and caused the solve/build to fail.

evidence.quote:
> [nydus 1/2] RUN wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz:
0.091 Connecting to github.com (140.82.113.3:443)
0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Full build step output showing the failed connection and 502 response from GitHub for that exact release file.

suggested_fix: Retry the job (transient network/502). If failures persist, make the dependency available via a stable cache or vendor it into the repo/build context, or add retry logic to the Dockerfile download (e.g. wget/curl with --retry and backoff) or use an alternate mirror/URL for the nydus release.
